text: Is the SAA posted with the LBNL approved SAA label (yellow sign) that includes the name and contact information of the person responsible for this SAA?
Are all in-progress waste containers properly labeled with the hazardous material names, owner’s name, and accumulation start date?
Are all waste containers ready for requisition properly labeled with a fully completed WMS label or Yellow RAD label for radioactive waste?
Is an up-to-date waste log maintained, including chemical names and concentrations?
Have all previous chemical RFID tags been removed from containers before they are reused for waste collection?
Is all radioactive waste properly labeled?
Are liquids wastes stored in secondary containment?
Are incompatible wastes physically separated with different secondary containments?
Are all flammable wastes greater than 1 quart in volume stored in an approved flammable waste container?
Are all waste containers dated within 6 months (180 days) of the 'SAA Accumulation Start Date'?
Are all waste containers closed when not in use (includes ensuring ventilation attachment parts are detached from flammable waste containers when not in use)?
Are the acutely toxic wastes (if any) in your SAA less than 1 quart in volume?
Are all wastes in your SAA less than 55 gallons in volume total?
Sharps containers should be labeled 'Unregulated sharps' and not be overfilled.
Bio-waste containers should not be overfilled (the container should be emptied when full and the lid should be closed at all times).
Biohazard labels should be clearly posted on waste container.
